behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: AI judgment-quality error (model outputs wrong signature despite correct prompt). Required setup: call the real LLM with interface-constrained prompts and validate parsed signature against expected interface. MOCK-MISS/REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess beyond normal API call is the core boundary. LLM-DEP: yes, correctness depends on real model output and can vary run-to-run. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because this behavior is a single generation/evaluation step, not multi-step agentic chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Run real LLM generation for a set of interface-sensitive prompts, parse generated function signatures, and assert exact match on parameter names/types/return type; fail on any mismatch.

behaviors.description: The generated implementation passes all provided test cases on the first or a subsequent attempt.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff/wiring error in generate->test->retry orchestration (e.g., retry not triggered, success state not propagated). Required setup: wire orchestrator, test-runner module, and LLM client with deterministic stubs that simulate fail-then-pass. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess is stated; (b) behavior can be validated with deterministic outputs, so real AI quality is not required; (c) no requirement for multiple real agentic steps. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text allows first or subsequent success and does not require evaluating real-model quality, only loop correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Use stub LLM outputs (attempt 1 fails tests, attempt 2 passes) and a real orchestrator to assert the service retries correctly and returns passing implementation/result.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement step includes the failure messages in the next LLM prompt so the model can learn from its mistakes.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff/wiring error (test failure output not injected into next prompt). Required setup: connect test execution result producer to prompt-construction + LLM-call module and inspect sent prompt payload. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed to expose this; (b) no dependence on real AI output quality; (c) no multi-step real agentic requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate a failed test run with concrete failure text, trigger refinement, and assert the next LLM request payload includes those failure messages verbatim/structured as specified.

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: orchestration/state aggregation error (attempt counter, termination condition, or result collection incorrect). Required setup: orchestrator + attempt store/error formatter with failing test outcomes across attempts. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess boundary in behavior text; (b) no real AI quality dependency if failures are stubbed deterministically; (c) not a multi-agentic quality chain requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Force three deterministic failed attempts via stubs and assert exactly three executions occur, generation stops, and returned error contains all three attempt artifacts and test results in schema.

behaviors.description: The full loop — generate, test, refine, test again — produces a working implementation for a function that a single LLM call typically gets wrong.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: multi-step orchestration error plus AI judgment-quality dependency (initial miss, feedback incorporation, improved second attempt). Required setup: real end-to-end loop with real LLM across multiple sequential agentic steps and real test execution to confirm final correctness. MULTI-STEP: explicitly required. LLM-DEP: explicitly required because success depends on refinement quality of real model outputs. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text explicitly requires the full multi-step loop, not a single model call.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end scenario on a known hard prompt where single-call baseline usually fails; execute real generate->test->refine->test loop and assert eventual pass plus evidence that refinement used prior failure context.
